canonical_definition: Based on the provided sources, ESConv is a benchmark dataset for emotional support conversations. It is defined as a collection of approximately 1,000 conversations between a 'help-seeker' and a 'supporter', with utterances annotated according to eight distinct support strategies. The dataset is used to measure text generation capabilities, specifically for evaluating performance in 'downstream response generation' within an emotional support context. One study reports using ESConv to demonstrate that its proposed framework 'excels at providing emotional support as well as diversifying support strategy selection'.
papers: Multilingual Language Model Pretraining using Machine-translated Data

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for emotional support conversations, collected in a help-seeker and supporter mode, containing approximately 1K conversations with utterances annotated with one of eight support strategies.

relationships.paper: Multilingual Language Model Pretraining using Machine-translated Data
relationships.evidence: “We conduct experiments on the ESConv dataset by selectively omitting each of the four distinct aspects when applying the IntentionFrame framework to enhance downstream response generation.”

emnlp25: Multilingual Language Model Pretraining using Machine-translated Data
